mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction that reduces consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and industrial production confirms broad-based demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield a 20-day forward return hit rate below 50%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 310 index threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 310, or the signal predictive power vanishes in a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO momentum, or Rank IC stays below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor market expansion that boosts household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll surges above 200k are followed by negative 20-day equity returns in > 60% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real GDP confirms synchronized expansion across residential and aggregate demand sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and GDPC1 signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a hit rate above 52%, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate drop below 2.0% triggers a liquidity injection that lowers discount rates and risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative or flat when FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0%, or the signal hit rate drops below 50% in walk-forward validation.
